article.title: Topical Spray of dsRNA Induces Mortality and Inhibits Chilli Leaf Curl Virus Transmission by Bemisia tabaci Asia II 1

events.event_label: dsRNA delivered to B. tabaci via artificial diet feeding setup
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p052.s012: Bacterially expressed dsRNA is economical and convenient, as reported in several studies. PMC8909865.p052.s013: Before evaluating the efficacy of topically sprayed dsRNA, we tested it under controlled laboratory conditions. PMC8909865.p052.s014: Both hsp70 and fas2 dsRNA were orally delivered to B. tabaci adults by mixing with an artificial diet in a feeding setup. PMC8909865.p052.s015: Similar types of artificial feeding setup were reported to be efficient in the oral delivery of dsRNA. PMC8909865.p052.s016: A significant knockdown of the B. tabaci population was recorded at 24 h, which gradually increased with an increase in dsRNA concentration.

events.event_label: Topical spray of dsRNA induces mortality and inhibits virus transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p001.s001: Topical Spray of dsRNA Induces Mortality and Inhibits Chilli Leaf Curl Virus Transmission by Bemisia tabaci Asia II 1

events.event_label: Foliar application of naked dsRNA reduces ChiLCV transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p002.s006: However, ChiLCV copies significantly increased in fas2 dsRNA-fed B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p002.s007: Transmission of ChiLCV by B. tabaci was completely inhibited post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA at 3 μg/mL. Naked hsp70 dsRNA was topically sprayed on ChiLCV-infected chilli plants like an insecticide. PMC8909865.p002.s008: 67.77% mortality of B. tabaci, 4.6-fold downregulation of hsp70 mRNA, and 1.34 × 1015-fold decreased ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci were recorded when adults were exposed to the dsRNA-treated plants under semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p002.s009: Foliar application of naked dsRNA reduced the ChiLCV transmission by 75% without any visible symptoms in the inoculated plants. PMC8909865.p002.s010: A total of 2 consecutive sprays of dsRNA provided significant protection to B. tabaci for up to 20 days under semi-field conditions.

events.event_label: hsp70‑targeting dsRNA induces mortality and inhibits ChiLCV transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p051.s009: RNAi using dsRNA constructs is not suitable for field conditions due to rapid degradation and a lack of reliable delivery agents. PMC8909865.p051.s010: In the present study, for the first time, we demonstrated the efficacy of a naked dsRNA against an insect pest by spray-on application under semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p051.s011: The dsRNA construct targeting the hsp70 gene of B. tabaci not only induced mortality in B. tabaci but also inhibited the transmission of ChiLCV from infected to healthy chilli plants by B. tabaci.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA protects chilli plants from B. tabaci and ChiLCV, reducing yield losses
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p055.s006: Infection of ChiLCV at the seedling stage leads to a considerable reduction in plant height, internodal length, and fruiting. PMC8909865.p055.s007: Hence, a 20-day shield by 2 consecutive sprays of naked hsp70 dsRNA would protect the crucial phase of crop growth from the invasion of B. tabaci and ChiLCV and reduce yield losses. PMC8909865.p055.s008: The foliar spray of hsp70 dsRNA was effective under semi-field conditions where the daily temperature fluctuated between 20–35 °C with 40–60% RH. PMC8909865.p055.s009: The efficacy and persistency of topically sprayed naked dsRNA at a wide temperature range endorse its potentiality for field uses as a spray-on technology.

events.event_label: TYLCV CP interacts with hsp70 in midgut epithelial cells
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p052.s003: Hsp70 transcripts increased upon ingestion of tomato yellow leaf curl virus (TYLCV) and squash leaf curl virus (SLCV) in DNA microarray. PMC8909865.p052.s004: The role of hsp70 in begomovirus transmission is well studied. PMC8909865.p052.s005: TYLCV CP and Hsp70 interacted in vitro and co-localized within midgut epithelial cells. PMC8909865.p052.s006: The fas2 mRNA expression level was upregulated in viruliferous B. tabaci by 2.6 and 4.518-fold in the RNA-Seq and RT-qPCR assay, respectively. PMC8909865.p052.s007: The Fas2 orthologue in mammals (NCAM) is known to serve as a receptor for the rabies virus.

events.event_label: Feeding on hsp70 dsRNA inhibits ChiLCV transmission

Naked hsp70 dsRNA was topically sprayed on ChiLCV-infected chilli plants like an insecticide. PMC8909865.p002.s008: 67.77% mortality of B. tabaci, 4.6-fold downregulation of hsp70 mRNA, and 1.34 × 1015-fold decreased ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci were recorded when adults were exposed to the dsRNA-treated plants under semi-field conditions. events.event_label: Hsp70 interacts with TYLCV CP and co‑localizes in midgut epithelial cells
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p053.s003: Hsp70, along with other molecular chaperones, plays an important role in the translocation of viral proteins besides its function in viral replication, assembly, and disassembly of the viral proteins inside the hosts. PMC8909865.p053.s004: Hsp70 has been found to interact with TYLCV CP in vitro and co-localize within midgut epithelial cells. PMC8909865.p053.s005: In the present study, oral delivery of hsp70 dsRNA significantly reduced ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci Asia II 1 and its transmission to healthy chilli plants as well. PMC8909865.p053.s006: The resistance to ChiLCV in B. tabaci was enhanced at higher concentrations of hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p053.s007: ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci were imperceptible when B. tabaci fed on 3 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA for 24 h. The transmission of ChiLCV from infected to healthy plants by B. tabaci Asia II 1 was completely inhibited at 3 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA treatment under controlled conditions.

events.event_label: Silencing hsp70 and fas2 affects mortality and virus transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p006.s003: The objective of the present study was to induce resistance against B. tabaci and ChiLCV by spray-on application of dsRNA. PMC8909865.p006.s004: In the present study, B. tabaci hsp70 and fas2 were silenced and resultant effects in mortality and virus transmission were reported. PMC8909865.p006.s005: For the first time, the efficacy of spray-on application of naked dsRNA against a hemipteran insect was demonstrated under semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p006.s006: The outcomes of the study provide a novel, eco-friendly option for protection against B. tabaci as well as ChiLCV, which can reduce economic losses caused by the virus-vector complex.

events.event_label: dsRNA is processed by Dicer which interacts with RISC
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p005.s012: Considering the continued lack of consumer acceptance, transgenic plant technology is not a feasible strategy. PMC8909865.p005.s013: In recent years, RNA interference (RNAi) has shown promise in the management of insect pests and plant viruses. PMC8909865.p005.s014: Double-stranded RNA (dsRNA), upon entering the host cell, is processed by the Dicer enzyme and, in association with RNA-induced silencing complex (RISC), cleaves the targeted mRNA. PMC8909865.p005.s015: Interrupting the interrelationship between B. tabaci and begomovirus using RNAi is a promising approach.. For successful persistent-circulative transmission, the begomovirus particles need to cross the midgut barrier of B. tabaci to circulate in hemolymph and reach the primary salivary glands. PMC8909865.p005.s016: Several proteins at the midguts of B. tabaci, such as heat shock proteins (Hsp), cyclophilins, peptidoglycan recognition protein, and a midgut protein, are known to interact with begomovirus coat protein (CP) for successful internalization.

events.event_label: dsRNA treatment reduces hsp70 expression, increases mortality and inhibits transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p054.s009: Hence, naked hsp70 dsRNA without any delivery agent was topically sprayed onto the foliage of the ChiLCV-infected chilli plant under contained semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p054.s010: B. tabaci adults were released on the dsRNA-sprayed plants to evaluate the dsRNA’s efficacy in eradicating B. tabaci and inhibiting the transmission of ChiLCV. PMC8909865.p054.s011: Considering the extracellular degradation of dsRNA and delivery through the host plant, a 10-fold higher dose than the controlled assay was used for topical application. PMC8909865.p054.s012: A 67.77% mortality with a 4.6-fold decrease in hsp70 mRNA levels in B. tabaci exposed to the dsRNA-sprayed plants was noted after 24 h of release. PMC8909865.p054.s013: Further, 1.84 × 108-fold decreased ChiLCV copies and 75% reduced transmission of ChiLCV indicated the efficiency of topically sprayed hsp70 dsRNA in managing both the virus and its vector.

events.event_label: Insecticides are used to manage B. tabaci and ChiLCV
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p051.s001: Several management strategies have been adopted around the world, with insecticides as the core component for the management of the B. tabaci-ChiLCV complex. PMC8909865.p051.s002: However, the use of chemical insecticides has led to several detrimental effects like the development of insecticide-resistant B. tabaci population, destruction of beneficial organisms, environmental contamination, and health hazards. PMC8909865.p051.s003: There is a need for an alternative eco-friendly approach in managing B. tabaci-ChiLCV. PMC8909865.p051.s004: RNAi or post-transcriptional gene silencing (PTGS) was introduced to study the functionality of specific genes through knocking down target gene mRNA.

events.event_label: DNA-A component detected in chilli plants and matches ChiLCV isolates
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p035.s001: The identity of the virus was confirmed by sequencing the DNA-A component from infected chilli plants. PMC8909865.p035.s002: PCR amplification of the DNA-A using Begomo F and Begomo R primer produced a 2.7 kb product visualized on 1% agarose gel. PMC8909865.p035.s003: Bidirectional sequencing of the cloned products produced a 2763 nt sequence comprising complete DNA-A that showed 100% nucleotide identity to ChiLCV isolates upon BLASTn analysis. PMC8909865.p035.s004: The sequence can be retrieved by GenBank Accession No. OM513903.

events.event_label: hsp70 and fas2 fragments generate putative siRNA
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p037.s001: A conserved 128 nt-long (2416 to 2543 nt) fragment of the hsp70 gene (~2.5 kb) and a 150 nt-long (1085 to 1234 nt) fragment of the fas2 gene (~1.4 kb) of B. tabaci were selected for dsRNA designing (Figure 1a,b). PMC8909865.p037.s002: In siRNA Wizard 3.1, the 128 nt-long B. tabaci hsp70 fragment produced a putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GAUCCAUCCAUGCCGUUAAUC-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s003: The 150 nt-long fas2 fragment also yielded one putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GGACGGAAGAAGGCAUAAUCA-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s004: The dsRNA sequence was specific to B. tabaci, and no cross-reactivity was recorded with other organisms such as Homo sapiens, Mus musculus, Aves (taxid:8782), Lepidoptera (taxid:7088), Hymenoptera (taxid:7399), Formicidae (taxid:36668), and plants (taxid:3193) in BLAST analysis.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA dose-dependently increases B. tabaci mortality
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p039.s003: Mortality of 67.77% of B. tabaci was observed at 24 h upon feeding on 1.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p039.s004: The mortality of B. tabaci significantly increased with an increase in dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p039.s005: The mean mortality was 70.0% when hsp70 dsRNA was fed to B. tabaci adults at 2.0 μg/mL, and it increased significantly up to 82.22% at 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA (Figure 2b). PMC8909865.p039.s006: Oral delivery of fas2 dsRNA at 1.0 μg/mL exhibited 39.33% mean mortality after 24 h (Figure 2b). PMC8909865.p039.s007: Significantly higher mortality (62 and 72%) of B. tabaci adults was recorded when 2.0 and 3.0 μg/mL fas2 dsRNA was orally delivered to B. tabaci under controlled laboratory conditions.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA feeding reduces hsp70 mRNA expression in B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p040.s001: RT-qPCR analysis showed that hsp70 dsRNA significantly reduced the hsp70 mRNA expression levels in treated B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p040.s002: Hsp70 mRNA levels in adult B. tabaci were decreased by 5.43-fold with respect to an endogenous control gene, β-actin, post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA (Figure 2c). PMC8909865.p040.s003: The mRNA expression levels were significantly reduced with an increase in hsp70 dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p040.s004: An exposure to hsp70 dsRNA at 2.0 μg/mL for 24 h significantly reduced the hsp70 mRNA expression by 11.32-fold and up to 12.85-fold when fed with 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA reduces ChiLCV copy number in B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p042.s003: The virus copies acquired by the B. tabaci were quantified by fitting the mean CT values obtained in qPCR into the standard curve. PMC8909865.p042.s004: The specificity of the reactions was confirmed by a single peak at 81 °C in the melting curve analysis of qPCR (Figure 3b). PMC8909865.p042.s005: The virus copy number acquired by hsp70 dsRNA-treated B. tabaci was significantly less than the untreated control. PMC8909865.p042.s006: When B. tabaci was fed with sterile distilled water instead of hsp70 dsRNA, the mean ChiLCV copy number was 2.22 × 1015, whereas it decreased by 35.18-fold (6.31 × 1013 copies) post-exposure to hsp70 dsRNA at 1.0 μg/mL (Figure 3c). PMC8909865.p042.s007: The virus copy number in treated B. tabaci further decreased with a gradual increase in hsp70 dsRNA concentration.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA reduces ChiLCV transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p159.s001: Effects of hsp70 and fas2 dsRNA in relation to ChiLCV acquisition by B. tabaci and respective involvement of hsp70 dsRNA in transmission. PMC8909865.p159.s002: (a) Standard curve of ChiLCV shows a linear relationship between log DNA concentrations in ng on the X-axis and × values on the Y-axis. PMC8909865.p159.s003: Each concentration was replicated thrice. PMC8909865.p159.s004: The equation of the straight line and the coefficient of correlation (R2) are mentioned on the graph.

events.event_label: topical spray of hsp70 dsRNA reduces B. tabaci mortality and ChiLCV transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p160.s001: Effect of topical spray of naked hsp70 dsRNA on mortality and ChiLCV transmission by Bemisia tabaci. PMC8909865.p160.s002: dsRNA was not directly applied on B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p160.s003: The naked hsp70 dsRNA was sprayed onto the foliage of ChiLCV-infected chilli plants like an insecticide at 30 μg/mL. B. tabaci adults were released on the dsRNA-treated plants. PMC8909865.p160.s004: (a) Percent mortality of adult B. tabaci post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA-treated plants.

events.event_label: topical dsRNA spray modulates B. tabaci mortality over time
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p049.s003: Significant mortality (56.67%) persisted up to 8 days after the first spray (Figure 5b). PMC8909865.p049.s004: The mortality decreased to 13.33% at 10 days after the first spray. PMC8909865.p049.s005: A second spray was done on the 10th day to check the effect of multiple sprays. PMC8909865.p049.s006: After the second topical spray of dsRNA, the mortality percentage again rose to 75% at two days. PMC8909865.p049.s007: The highest mortality (86.67%) of B. tabaci was recorded 4 days after the second spray.

events.event_label: dsRNA treatment decreases hsp70 abundance and affects B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p002.s006: However, ChiLCV copies significantly increased in fas2 dsRNA-fed B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p002.s007: Transmission of ChiLCV by B. tabaci was completely inhibited post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA at 3 μg/mL. Naked hsp70 dsRNA was topically sprayed on ChiLCV-infected chilli plants like an insecticide. PMC8909865.p002.s008: 67.77% mortality of B. tabaci, 4.6-fold downregulation of hsp70 mRNA, and 1.34 × 1015-fold decreased ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci were recorded when adults were exposed to the dsRNA-treated plants under semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p002.s009: Foliar application of naked dsRNA reduced the ChiLCV transmission by 75% without any visible symptoms in the inoculated plants. PMC8909865.p002.s010: A total of 2 consecutive sprays of dsRNA provided significant protection to B. tabaci for up to 20 days under semi-field conditions.

events.event_label: topical spray of dsRNA modulates B. tabaci mortality over time
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p049.s001: To check the persistency in eradicating B. tabaci population by the topical spray of hsp70 dsRNA under contained semi-field conditions, fresh B. tabaci adults were released on the dsRNA-treated chilli plants at 24 h intervals. PMC8909865.p049.s002: After the first topical spray, mortality of 58.89% was recorded at 2 days. PMC8909865.p049.s003: Significant mortality (56.67%) persisted up to 8 days after the first spray (Figure 5b). PMC8909865.p049.s004: The mortality decreased to 13.33% at 10 days after the first spray. PMC8909865.p049.s005: A second spray was done on the 10th day to check the effect of multiple sprays. PMC8909865.p049.s006: After the second topical spray of dsRNA, the mortality percentage again rose to 75% at two days. PMC8909865.p049.s007: The highest mortality (86.67%) of B. tabaci was recorded 4 days after the second spray.

events.event_label: dsRNA spray detected in leaves and reduces yield losses, B. tabaci and ChiLCV
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p055.s001: Topical application of dsRNA could provide resistance for 12 to 21 days against plant viruses. PMC8909865.p055.s002: In the present study, dsRNA was detected in the leaf tissues up to 6 h post-application. PMC8909865.p055.s003: Significant mortality of B. tabaci on the treated plants persisted up to 8 days and was further sustained up to 20 days with a second spray on the 10th day. PMC8909865.p055.s004: Protection of the plants during the early growth stage is crucial in the management of begomoviruses. PMC8909865.p055.s006: Infection of ChiLCV at the seedling stage leads to a considerable reduction in plant height, internodal length, and fruiting. PMC8909865.p055.s007: Hence, a 20-day shield by 2 consecutive sprays of naked hsp70 dsRNA would protect the crucial phase of crop growth from the invasion of B. tabaci and ChiLCV and reduce yield losses. PMC8909865.p055.s008: The foliar spray of hsp70 dsRNA was effective under semi-field conditions where the daily temperature fluctuated between 20–35 °C with 40–60% RH. PMC8909865.p055.s009: The efficacy and persistency of topically sprayed naked dsRNA at a wide temperature range endorse its potentiality for field uses as a spray-on technology.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA decreases_abundance_of virus copy number
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p042.s006: When B. tabaci was fed with sterile distilled water instead of hsp70 dsRNA, the mean ChiLCV copy number was 2.22 × 1015, whereas it decreased by 35.18-fold (6.31 × 1013 copies) post-exposure to hsp70 dsRNA at 1.0 μg/mL (Figure 3c). PMC8909865.p042.s007: The virus copy number in treated B. tabaci further decreased with a gradual increase in hsp70 dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p042.s008: At 2.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA, ChiLCV copy numbers were 6.14 × 1010 in B. tabaci i.e., 3.62 × 104-fold lower than the untreated control. PMC8909865.p042.s009: The virus copies were undetectable in B. tabaci with a further increase in hsp70 dsRNA concentration to 3.0 μg/mL. The retention of ChiLCV might be completely ceased at a higher concentration of hsp70 dsRNA or beyond the detection limit using qPCR. PMC8909865.p042.s010: In contrast, the ChiLCV copy number acquired by fas2 dsRNA-treated B. tabaci was significantly higher than the untreated control (Figure 3d).

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA affects transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p043.s001: A similar pattern was recorded in the transmission of ChiLCV from infected to healthy chilli plants by hsp70 dsRNA-fed B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p043.s002: A 100% transmission of ChiLCV was recorded when B. tabaci was not exposed to hsp70 dsRNA in the control set. PMC8909865.p043.s003: All the inoculated plants produced strong leaf curl and vein-clearing symptoms and tested ChiLCV positive in PCR. PMC8909865.p043.s004: Although there was a significant decrease in ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci exposed to 1.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA, all the test plants were found to be infected with ChiLCV in PCR testing at 35 days post-inoculation.

events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p053.s003: Hsp70, along with other molecular chaperones, plays an important role in the translocation of viral proteins besides its function in viral replication, assembly, and disassembly of the viral proteins inside the hosts. PMC8909865.p053.s007: ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci were imperceptible when B. tabaci fed on 3 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA for 24 h. PMC8909865.p053.s011: In the present study, the inability of B. tabaci Asia II 1 to retain and transmit ChiLCV post-silencing of hsp70 indicates that it might also play an important function in ChiLCV infection. PMC8909865.p053.s012: However, Gotz et al. reported an increase in TYLCV transmission upon blocking Hsp70 by anti-Hsp70 antibody in B. tabaci MEAM1. PMC8909865.p002.s007: Transmission of ChiLCV by B. tabaci was completely inhibited post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA at 3 μg/mL. Naked hsp70 dsRNA was topically sprayed on ChiLCV-infected chilli plants like an insecticide. PMC8909865.p002.s008: 67.77% mortality of B. tabaci, 4.6-fold downregulation of hsp70 mRNA, and 1.34 × 1015-fold decreased ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci were recorded when adults were exposed to the dsRNA-treated plants under semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p005.s014: Double-stranded RNA (dsRNA), upon entering the host cell, is processed by the Dicer enzyme and, in association with RNA-induced silencing complex (RISC), cleaves the targeted mRNA. PMC8909865.p005.s015: Interrupting the interrelationship between B. tabaci and begomovirus using RNAi is a promising approach.. For successful persistent-circulative transmission, the begomovirus particles need to cross the midgut barrier of B. tabaci to circulate in hemolymph and reach the primary salivary glands. PMC8909865.p005.s016: Several proteins at the midguts of B. tabaci, such as heat shock proteins (Hsp), cyclophilins, peptidoglycan recognition protein, and a midgut protein, are known to interact with begomovirus coat protein (CP) for successful internalization.

relations.triple: sterile distilled water changes_abundance_of ChiLCV copy number
relations.subject: sterile distilled water

evidence.text: Although several potential RNAi targets were identified for insects, including B. tabaci, most of those studies were conducted under controlled experimental conditions, and their efficacy under field conditions is limited. Rapid degradation of dsRNA in the extracellular environment and lack of reliable dsRNA delivery technique limits its applicability under field conditions. The objective of the present study was to induce resistance against B. tabaci and ChiLCV by spray-on application of dsRNA. In the present study, B. tabaci hsp70 and fas2 were silenced and resultant effects in mortality and virus transmission were reported. For the first time, the efficacy of spray-on application of naked dsRNA against a hemipteran insect was demonstrated under semi-field conditions. The outcomes of the study provide a novel, eco-friendly option for protection against B. tabaci as well as ChiLCV, which can reduce economic losses caused by the virus-vector complex.

entities.selected_description: Any biological process that results in permanent cessation of all vital functions of a cell. A cell should be considered dead when any one of the following molecular or morphological criteria is met: (1) the cell has lost the integrity of its plasma membrane; (2) the cell, including its nucleus, has undergone complete fragmentation into discrete bodies (frequently referred to as apoptotic bodies). The cell corpse (or its fragments) may be engulfed by an adjacent cell in vivo, but engulfment of whole cells should not be considered a strict criteria to define cell death as, under some circumstances, live engulfed cells can be released from phagosomes (see PMID:18045538).
